series.title: [v2] hw/riscv/riscv-iommu: Use standard EN_PRI bit for PRI

review.summary: The change is narrow and appears semantically correct: PRI enablement switches from a temporary custom TC[32] bit to the standard RISCV_IOMMU_DC_TC_EN_PRI definition, matching the stated spec alignment without altering the surrounding control flow.

stages.A: Patch intent is clear and self-consistent: replace a temporary guest-visible control bit with the standardized PRI enable bit.
stages.B: Code-path review of the touched logic shows no functional change beyond the selected bit mask used to gate automatic page-request generation when IOTLB permission is IOMMU_NONE.
stages.C: I did not identify missing follow-up changes in the shown path; enable_pid and ATS handling remain unchanged, so the patch preserves existing behavior apart from the intended bit definition update.
stages.D: Reported metadata is favorable but limited: checkpatch is clean and configure passes, though no compile or runtime test result is provided.
stages.E: No blocking issues found in the submitted diff. The patch is suitable for merging as a spec-alignment cleanup.
